programmes.show_description: Too Cold for Hell: While tracking a local conman, Frank and Lu make some unlikely allies when they encounter a pair of deadly gangsters.
A Game of Soldiers: A feature-length mystery for the detectives. The apparently motiveless murder of an American tourist brings Dalziel back into contact with an old flame.
Parting: Time for a cuppa with Chigwell's most famous sisters. Sharon is faced with an impossible choice when her husband, Chris, tells her he is seriously ill.
The Hope Too Bright to Last: Corcoran finds himself at an unusual dead end. Doctor Freeman is faced with the outbreak of a new and troubling illness sweeping Five Points.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Jobs for the Girls: Chigwell comedy with the Essex girls. Sharon and Tracey want spending money for Christmas, so they take up some extra work to pay for last minute presents.
Walking the Line: The normally moderate Duncan Lennox is outraged to be accused of racism by a suspected robber.
New. Stabbed in the Back: Daniel steps in to save the surgery when an angry patient threatens to drive a truck into the building after discovering his wife's affair.
New. Eye of the Beholder: When Michelle visits a reclusive spinster, she discovers the woman has been hiding a son with a facial deformity.
Pastures New: Kath decides to leave Holby - but before going, she admits to Lisa that she helped Terry die. Mubbs lashes out at one of Rosie's daughters, and George gets another new heart.
Entry Wounds Part 2: The aftermath of the shootings and the dramatic incidents at Holby College leave everyone in the emergency department struggling to cope.
Walking the Line: The normally moderate Duncan Lennox is outraged to be accused of racism by a suspected robber.
New. Bradley is terrified the police will discover his secret, while Shirley wonders if she can trust Phil. Bianca is excited with wedding preparations as surprise guests arrive.
New. The police receive an anonymous tip-off, but will they arrest the right person? Ian's suspicious behaviour raises questions for Lucy, and Max worries about Jack's plan.
Only One Woof: Peter Davison, the fifth Doctor Who, stars in the veterinary drama. James and Siegfried deal with a sheepdog trainer whose dog is suffering from epilepsy.
Jan is surprised to learn she has a business rival, and The Orkadian has its official launch at the Mermaid Yard - without Jack.
How The Rogue Roar'd: When Frank and Lu are hired to help close down an old bingo hall, Frank is surprised to find an old nemesis at work.
Just Visiting: Sharon is rejoicing in her new-found freedom, while Tracey is lost without Darryl and is wondering how she is going to break the news of his crime to their son Garth.
The Genuine Outdoors Robin Hood Barbi: Roy Clarke's record breaking sitcom. Billy takes Clegg, Truly, Alvin and Entwistle out for a real barbecue in the real outdoors.
Barry in Danger from Reading and Aunt Jessie: The sitcom that's long in the tooth but has a spring in its step! Nora Batty enquires about the mystery woman seen with Alvin.
Who's That Merry Man with Billy, Then?: The world's longest-running sitcom! Recruitment for Billy's Merry Men is at a low ebb, until he receives a letter from an enthusiast.
Girl, Whitstable Beach: After seven years, a missing young mum is spotted on Whitstable Beach. Pearl wants to know where she's gone and why she's back.
Death, the Devil and the Fool: When a young girl goes missing at the Whitstable Psychic Fair, a sceptical Pearl has to join forces with a clairvoyant to help find her.
Amanda Redman and Alun Armstrong star in the light-hearted crime series. When Gerry loses badly at poker, he agrees to pay his debt by investigating the death of a bookie.
How The Rogue Roar'd: When Frank and Lu are hired to help close down an old bingo hall, Frank is surprised to find an old nemesis at work.
The Price of Fame: An involving mystery from the award-winning mind of Reginald Hill. When an entertainer's body is washed up on a beach, Pascoe faces a case without Dalziel.
Jobs for the Girls: Chigwell comedy with the Essex girls. Sharon and Tracey want spending money for Christmas, so they take up some extra work to pay for last minute presents.
Ashes Denote That Fire Was: The greater Copper family, uptown and Five Points alike, gathers around Corcoran, who is reeling from a loss. Freeman steps beyond his role as doctor.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Someone Else's Baby: More laughs with the Essex sisters. Sharon and Tracey decide to search through their mother's belongings for undiscovered treasure.
Cover Stories: Burnside discovers Carver's drinking habit, but he has secrets of his own to protect.
New. And the Winner Is: Julia secretly enters Vivien into a recipe competition but the cautious cook is reluctant to give an interview when she emerges as the winner.
New. Searching: Nick helps a girl in care who refuses to accept that her mother is dead. Meanwhile, Eva tells Jimmi she has asked for a transfer.
Past Caring: Ed admits to Tom that he's being blackmailed, and Ric is unimpressed to find Robert has been readmitted, but feels more sympathetic as he discovers the extent of his injuries.
The Blame Game: Jordan bears the brunt of an attack on the ED from the Press and public, while Ruth eagerly awaits news regarding her application for a surgical job.
Cover Stories: Burnside discovers Carver's drinking habit, but he has secrets of his own to protect.
New. With Bianca and Ricky making their way to the church, Carol's appearance is in danger of ruining the wedding celebrations. Meanwhile, Janine is determined to get revenge.
New. At the Vic, everyone toasts the couple. Becca sneaks upstairs to return Ronnie's hairbrush.
Ace, King, Queen, Jack: Nostalgic fun in the Great British series about vets. A hilarious episode as James is dreading the Darrowby dog show because of Mrs Pumphrey's dog, Tricky Woo.
Tom and Emma tell Jack about their intention to accept Charles' offer, while Polly and Gerald fall out over their business plans.
See Thyself, Devil!: When a washed-up rock star thinks the Devil is trying to kill him, Frank and Lu must uncover the truth before the real killer strikes again.
Shift!: Catch up with Chigwell's finest. Sharon is enjoying her new life in Tracey's home and helping herself to the prawns in the fridge, Tracey is having money troubles.
Who's That Talking to Lenny?: More hijinks with those young-at-heart OAPs. Lenny from the pickle factory hears a voice from above, and waits patiently for more information.
Oh Look! Mitzi's Found Her Mummy: Popular sitcom. Clegg looks after a friendly little dog for the day, but when Truly, Billy and Alvin arrive at his house, the animal turns ferocious.
Plenty of Room in the Back: More silliness with those sprightly OAPs. Auntie Wainwright admits that she needs a new means of transport and sends Tom and Smiler to collect it.
Sailing Close To The Wind Part 1: After a revered member of the sailing club is brutally bludgeoned to death, Judith, Becks and Suzie investigate.
Sailing Close To The Wind Part 2: Motives stack up against the suspects, Kit's killer sends another violent warning and Judith, Becks and Suzie are in a race against time.
Another adventure for the eccentric ex-detectives. The team re-opens the unsolved abduction of two 10-year-old boys. Gerry struggles to give up smoking.
